code:
class Solution:
    def minimumDeleteSum(self, s1: str, s2: str) -> int:
        m, n = len(s1), len(s2)

        # dp[i][j] will store the minimum ASCII delete sum for s1[:i] and s2[:j]
        # to make them equal.
        dp = [[0] * (n + 1) for _ in range(m + 1)]

        # Initialize base cases
        # If s1 is empty, we must delete all characters in s2 to make it empty.
        # The cost is the sum of ASCII values of characters in s2[:j].
        for j in range(1, n + 1):
            dp[0][j] = dp[0][j-1] + ord(s2[j-1])
        
        # If s2 is empty, we must delete all characters in s1 to make it empty.
        # The cost is the sum of ASCII values of characters in s1[:i].
        for i in range(1, m + 1):
            dp[i][0] = dp[i-1][0] + ord(s1[i-1])

        # Fill the DP table
        for i in range(1, m + 1):
            for j in range(1, n + 1):
                if s1[i-1] == s2[j-1]:
                    # If the last characters match, we don't need to delete them.
                    # The minimum delete sum for s1[:i] and s2[:j] is the same
                    # as for s1[:i-1] and s2[:j-1].
                    dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j-1]
                else:
                    # If the last characters don't match, we must delete one of them.
                    # Option 1: Delete s1[i-1]. The cost is ord(s1[i-1]) added to
                    #           the minimum delete sum for s1[:i-1] and s2[:j].
                    # Option 2: Delete s2[j-1]. The cost is ord(s2[j-1]) added to
                    #           the minimum delete sum for s1[:i] and s2[:j-1].
                    # We choose the option that results in the minimum total delete sum.
                    dp[i][j] = min(ord(s1[i-1]) + dp[i-1][j], 
                                   ord(s2[j-1]) + dp[i][j-1])
        
        return dp[m][n]
prompt: Minimum ASCII delete sum for two strings.
